title: Flowlink Sprint 03 — First Offer & Case Development
goal: Convert the most validated Flowlink workflow into a clear starter offer, define the structure of an early case study, and create a CTA that moves attention to conversations.

steps.title: Turn a Workflow Win Into an Offer People Can Understand

sections.body: Early offers should be built around a narrow result with an obvious before-and-after. Do not sell Flowlink as a broad platform. Sell '20 hours back' on a specific repurposing workflow.
sections.heading: The Outcome is the Offer

sections.body: A credible case narrative has five parts: starting state, workflow friction, intervention, changed process, and result.
sections.heading: Proof Before Polish

steps.title: Package the First Offer and Proof Path

objectives.description: Write a one-sentence offer naming audience, pain, and result.
Draft a simple transformation case narrative.
Define 3 qualifying questions for your CTA flow.

items.text: Outline the step-by-step deliverable for the first client.
What is the value-based price point for this outcome?

steps.title: What Would Make Someone Say No?

prompts.text: What would make a creator say 'no' to this offer right now?
What is the smallest 'win' you can deliver in the first 72 hours?

questions.question: Why is a narrow offer better than a platform pitch for early validation?
questions.expected_answer: Easier to understand, sell, and fulfill.
